title: interface instances should not be cast to concrete types
description: "interface" instances should not be cast to concrete typesAnalyze your codeadaptability-focusedmaintainabilityCode SmelldesignWhy is this an issue?Needing to cast from aninterfaceto a concrete type indicates that something is wrong with the abstractions in use, likely that something is missing from theinterface. Instead of casting to a discrete type, the missing functionality should be added to theinterface. Otherwise there is a risk of runtime exceptions.Noncompliant code examplepublic interface IMyInterface { void DoStuff(); } public class MyClass1 : IMyInterface { public int Data { get { return new Random().Next(); } } public void DoStuff() { // TODO..
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects casting from interface types to concrete class types
logic.triggering_conditions: (Explicit cast from interface to class) OR (Pattern matching 'is' from interface to class)
logic.reasoning: This rule detects poor abstraction design where interface instances are cast to concrete types. Pattern matches: (1) explicit casts like '(ConcreteClass)interfaceVar', (2) pattern matching 'if (iface is ConcreteClass)'. This indicates missing functionality in the interface. Excludes interface-to-interface casts which are valid. AST-based approach examines Assignment and MethodDeclaration nodes for cast expressions.
